questions.question: What does the == operator compare when used with two object references in Java?
options: The memory addresses (references) of the objects
The hash codes of the objects
The string representations of the objects
questions.answer: The memory addresses (references) of the objects

options: It depends on the JVM implementation

questions.question: Why does == return true for two String literals with the same content?
options: The == operator always compares String content
Both literals reference the same object in the String Pool
The compiler converts == to .equals() for Strings
questions.answer: Both literals reference the same object in the String Pool

questions.question: Why does runtime String concatenation using a variable produce a different result with == than concatenation of two literals?
options: Variables always create new String objects at runtime, bypassing the String Pool
The + operator works differently with variables
Literal concatenation is evaluated at compile time and placed in the String Pool, while variable concatenation creates a new object at runtime
Runtime concatenation uses a different character encoding
questions.answer: Literal concatenation is evaluated at compile time and placed in the String Pool, while variable concatenation creates a new object at runtime

questions.question: What does the equals() method return when comparing two Strings?
options: An int indicating their relative order
A boolean indicating whether their content is identical
A boolean indicating whether they reference the same object
questions.answer: A boolean indicating whether their content is identical

questions.question: What does "Apple".compareTo("Banana") return?
What does the compareTo() method return when two Strings are equal?
Why is "test".equals(nullStr) considered safer than nullStr.equals("test")?
options: The literal can never be null, so calling .equals() on it will not throw a NullPointerException
The compiler requires literals to be on the left
questions.answer: The literal can never be null, so calling .equals() on it will not throw a NullPointerException

options: false because they are different types

questions.question: What does "HELLO".compareTo("hello") return?
options: 0 because the letters are the same
A positive integer because uppercase comes after lowercase
A negative integer because uppercase letters have lower Unicode values
questions.answer: A negative integer because uppercase letters have lower Unicode values

questions.question: Which decision structure is most appropriate for mapping a numeric month (1-12) to its name?
Which decision structure is best suited for checking a single condition that requires one action if true and no action if false?
When should you use an else-if chain instead of a switch statement?
options: When you have exactly two conditions
When conditions involve ranges or complex boolean expressions rather than matching a single value against discrete options
When you want the code to run faster
questions.answer: When conditions involve ranges or complex boolean expressions rather than matching a single value against discrete options

questions.question: What is the correct way to validate that a user-entered account type matches "checking" regardless of case?
A year is a leap year if it is divisible by 4, except if divisible by 100, unless also divisible by 400. Which decision structure is most appropriate?
options: Nested if statements or an if-else chain with compound boolean expressions
questions.answer: Nested if statements or an if-else chain with compound boolean expressions

questions.question: Which of the following correctly uses the ternary operator to assign "Even" or "Odd" to a String variable?

options: Whether the word starts with a letter in the first half of the alphabet (A-M)
Whether the word is longer than the String "N"
questions.answer: Whether the word starts with a letter in the first half of the alphabet (A-M)

questions.question: In a bank account simulator, which decision structure is most appropriate for handling a menu with options 1 through 6?
What is the correct way to check if a String variable name is not empty after removing leading and trailing whitespace?
When using compareTo() to sort three names alphabetically, what does a negative return value from name1.compareTo(name2) indicate?
